title: Project-control CLI hardening (owner code-audit P0)
objective: Harden tools/project_control.py: explicit transition enum for progress (never sets accepted); accept requires awaiting_gate + all required gates PASS + all dependencies accepted + zero open blockers referencing the task; gate reviewer must be in reviewer_agents and differ from producer_agent (G2 evidence recorded by orchestrator documented as the sanctioned exception); task-ID and report-path validation contained under approved directories; atomic concurrency-safe writes; honest documentation that --agent is not cryptographic identity; negative tests for every prohibited transition and spoofing attempt
business_reason: Owner code-audit directive 2026-07-17 P0: control-plane integrity before further milestone throughput
inputs: Owner code-audit directive 2026-07-17 P0 project-control items (verbatim in project-control/reports/OWNER-REVIEW-code-audit-task-consolidation.md section 2.1)
tools/project_control.py current behavior (progress accepts free-form status; accept checks gates but not dependencies/blockers; gate rejects only reviewer == producer_agent)
tools/test_project_control.py existing regression suite (runs in the CI control-plane job)

.claude/rules/project-control.md ADR-005 + owner clarification 2026-07-17: G2 is structurally a SELF-CHECK, not independent review. Codify narrowly: G2/self-check evidence may be authored by the orchestrator; its actor role is recorded honestly as orchestrator/self_check; a self-check record can NEVER satisfy a gate requiring independent review (G1/G3/G4/G5/G6); all independent gates require a reviewer listed in reviewer_agents who differs from producer_agent; NO general reviewer-validation bypass exists
outputs: Hardened tools/project_control.py: progress restricted to an explicit transition enum and can never set accepted; accept requires status awaiting_gate AND every required gate PASS AND every dependency accepted AND zero open blocker records referencing the task; gate distinguishes gate classes structurally - self-check gates (G2) accept actor role orchestrator/self_check and record it honestly as such, independent-review gates (G1/G3/G4/G5/G6) require reviewer in reviewer_agents AND reviewer != producer_agent, and a self_check record can never satisfy an independent gate; task-ID format validation; report paths validated and contained under project-control/reports/; atomic writes (temp file + os.replace) safe under concurrent invocation
Honest documentation in the tool: --agent is caller-provided, not cryptographic identity; enforcement is procedural (ADR-005) plus these validations
Extended tools/test_project_control.py with a negative test for EVERY prohibited transition and spoofing attempt
forbidden_paths: project-control ledger data (state.json, tasks/, gates/, checkpoints/, blockers/) - the tool is edited, never the data
acceptance_scenarios: S1 transition enum: every legal lifecycle transition (backlog->ready->claimed->in_progress->self_check->awaiting_gate + rework/blocked paths per docs/GATES_AND_CHECKPOINTS.md) passes; progress with status accepted or any value outside the enum is REJECTED

S2 accept preconditions: accept fails when (a) status != awaiting_gate, (b) any required gate lacks PASS, (c) any dependency is not accepted, (d) an open blocker references the task; passes only when all four hold

S3 gate classes: independent gate with reviewer not in reviewer_agents rejected; reviewer == producer_agent rejected; G2 recorded with actor role orchestrator/self_check passes AND is stored with that honest role label; attempting to satisfy an independent gate with a self_check record is rejected; no code path provides a general reviewer-validation bypass - each rule has its own test
S4 containment: malformed task IDs and report paths outside project-control/reports/ (../ traversal, absolute paths, drive-letter escapes) rejected on every subcommand
S5 atomicity: concurrent progress writes to one task (threaded harness) never corrupt JSON; an interrupted write leaves the previous valid file intact
S6 spoofing negatives: producer accepting own task, producer gating own task, self-review via renamed --agent, progress jumping to 100 by a producer - all rejected, each with a test
S7 regression: all existing ledger files still parse; existing suite + CI control-plane job green (validate-on-write only; stored history is not retro-rejected)
risks: Backward compatibility with the 21-accepted ledger: validation applies on write, never retroactively
This tool is the orchestrator's own control plane - merge only after gates pass to avoid breaking mid-session ledger operations
